name: Commonwealth of Pennsylvania (Office of Administration / OA-OIT)
tagline: Pennsylvania's central IT shop: OA's Office for Information Technology sets statewide technology direction, runs shared services and cyber for executive agencies, and is the public face of the state's first-in-the-nation generative-AI push.
overview: The Office for Information Technology (OA-OIT) sits inside the Governor's Office of Administration and is the Commonwealth's central IT authority — it oversees the investment in and performance of IT across all executive agencies under the Governor's jurisdiction and sets the policies, standards, and security guidelines those agencies follow. OA-OIT runs enterprise shared services (infrastructure, cloud, data, identity), statewide cybersecurity, and the digital-experience portfolio, and it is the most visible state-government AI program in the country following Pennsylvania's OpenAI/ChatGPT Enterprise pilot. Leadership turned over in late 2025 and was stabilized in early 2026: Bry Pardoe became Commonwealth CIO in October 2025, and in February 2026 Secretary of Administration Neil Weaver made four interim OIT leaders permanent, including a new state CISO. Note the buying nuance — large agency IT budgets (DHS, PennDOT, etc.) live with those agencies; OA-OIT is the policy, standards, and shared-services center, while most cooperative purchasing flows through the separate Department of General Services (DGS) and its COSTARS program.

budget.intro: OA-OIT's leverage is in statewide direction, shared services, and security policy rather than a single large appropriation. Governor Shapiro's FY2026-27 proposed budget is $53.26B in General Fund spending (up ~5.4%), and OA's IT story this cycle is modernization savings plus a multi-year cybersecurity build-out.

stats.label: Tech savings (3 yr)
stats.note: From right-sizing IT contracts, cloud, and in-housing

notes: The FY2026-27 proposal directs additional funding to a multi-year OA cybersecurity modernization strategy against ransomware and data-theft threats.

OA reports ~$37M in technology savings over three years by right-sizing contracts, expanding cloud, and bringing work in-house (CODE PA alone ~$10M) — a cost-discipline frame Dell should expect in deals.
The largest IT spend sits in operating agencies (DHS, PennDOT, Revenue, etc.); OA-OIT is the policy and shared-services center, not the wallet for every project.

initiatives.name: Generative AI — ChatGPT Enterprise expansion
initiatives.summary: After a first-in-the-nation OpenAI pilot (175 employees, 14 agencies) that reported ~95 min/day saved, Pennsylvania expanded ChatGPT Enterprise to 3,000+ employees across 35 agencies in early 2026, with more in training. AI is a top-of-house priority — align infrastructure, data, and endpoint plays to it.

initiatives.name: Cybersecurity modernization
initiatives.summary: OA is pursuing a multi-year cyber modernization strategy, reinforced in the FY2026-27 proposal, to harden state systems against ransomware and data theft. A clear lane for secure infrastructure, endpoint, and zero-trust offerings under the new CISO.

initiatives.name: Cloud & IT cost optimization
initiatives.summary: OA credits ~$37M in three-year savings to right-sizing IT contracts, expanding cloud, and in-housing work. Expect ROI-driven, consolidation-minded buying — position hybrid cloud and as-a-service against measurable cost outcomes.

initiatives.name: Digital experience (CODE PA)
initiatives.summary: The Commonwealth Office of Digital Experience — led since Feb 2026 by Executive Director Sara Hall — marked three years (~$10M saved, five national awards) and in spring 2026 began rolling out four enterprise platforms: a centralized permits/licensing system, a grants one-stop shop, a modernized payment platform, and a single resident login. A direct pull-through for the data, identity, and infrastructure beneath citizen-facing services.

procurement.intro: Selling into Pennsylvania state government runs through pre-established contracts, not one-off RFPs per agency. Most cooperative purchasing is administered by the Department of General Services (DGS), not OA — confirm Dell's coverage on the right DGS statewide and COSTARS contracts, and register in the PA Supplier Portal.

vehicles.name: COSTARS (DGS cooperative)
vehicles.detail: Pennsylvania's cooperative purchasing program, run by DGS — local governments, school districts, and state-affiliated entities buy off DGS-established contracts (including IT hardware/software) without their own RFP. Thousands of members registered.

vehicles.name: DGS statewide contracts
vehicles.detail: Statewide contracts established by DGS that executive agencies and COSTARS members buy from — the primary path for commodity and enterprise IT acquisition.

vehicles.name: PA Supplier Portal registration
vehicles.detail: Prerequisite to bidding on statewide or COSTARS contracts — suppliers register to receive a vendor number before participating.

powerStructure.name: Neil Weaver
powerStructure.title: Secretary of Administration
powerStructure.note: Cabinet head over OA; oversees enterprise IT, HR, and digital services. The executive sponsor above the CIO.

powerStructure.name: Bry Pardoe
powerStructure.title: Commonwealth Chief Information Officer
powerStructure.note: Named CIO Oct 2025; owns IT for all executive agencies under the Governor. Came from CODE PA — UX- and AI-forward. The top IT relationship.

powerStructure.name: Jim Sipe
powerStructure.title: Executive Deputy CIO
powerStructure.note: Made permanent Feb 2026 (prior state CISO since 2023) — day-to-day delivery and operations leader under the CIO.

powerStructure.name: Andy Ritter
powerStructure.title: Chief Information Security Officer
powerStructure.note: Confirmed permanent state CISO Feb 2026; interim since Oct 2025 and deputy CISO before. Owns statewide security policy and the cyber-modernization buying center.

powerStructure.name: Greg Fiedler
powerStructure.title: Chief Technology Officer
powerStructure.note: Appointed CTO March 2026 — architecture, infrastructure, and platform direction.

news.title: Commonwealth CIO Bry Pardoe named to StateScoop 50 (GoldenGov) for the second straight year
news.impact: Confirms Pardoe remains the top IT decision-maker and reaffirms her stated priorities — zero-trust cyber, cloud-first legacy modernization, and shared digital infrastructure — the themes to anchor Dell's message to.

news.title: Code for America ranks Pennsylvania among national leaders for government AI readiness and impact
news.impact: Independent recognition (2026 Government AI Landscape Assessment) reinforces AI as a marquee statewide priority beyond the ChatGPT rollout — sustain AI-ready infrastructure and data conversations. (Exact publication month approximate.)

news.title: CODE PA marks three years; begins rolling out four enterprise platforms (permits, grants, payments, single resident login)
news.impact: New statewide citizen-service platforms are a direct pull-through for the data, identity, and infrastructure beneath them — position hybrid cloud and secure infrastructure behind the buildout.

news.title: CISO Andy Ritter details zero-trust, IAM, and vulnerability-management strategy (StateTech Q&A)
news.impact: Names the concrete security lanes OA is investing in — align zero-trust, identity, and secure-infrastructure offerings to Ritter's roadmap.

news.title: Pennsylvania makes four interim OIT leaders permanent, including new CISO Andy Ritter
news.impact: Leadership stabilizes after late-2025 turnover; clarifies who owns delivery (Sipe) and security (Ritter) for relationship mapping.

news.title: Shapiro FY2026-27 proposal strengthens OA cybersecurity funding
news.impact: Multi-year cyber-modernization funding (an added ~$10M) signals demand for secure infrastructure and endpoint — time security plays to the budget cycle.

dellActions: Confirm Dell's coverage on DGS statewide and COSTARS IT contracts and current PA Supplier Portal registration — that is the access path to executive agencies and the broader Pennsylvania public sector.
Anchor the motion to OA-OIT's generative-AI program: position AI-ready infrastructure (compute, storage, networking) and secure endpoints behind the ChatGPT Enterprise rollout.
Lead cybersecurity offerings — secure infrastructure, endpoint, and zero-trust — into the FY2026-27 multi-year cyber-modernization funding, with CISO Andy Ritter's office.
Frame deals around measurable cost outcomes (cloud optimization, contract right-sizing) to match OA's $37M-savings, in-housing-minded posture.
Rebuild the relationship map after the 2025-26 leadership turnover: CIO Bry Pardoe, Exec Deputy CIO Jim Sipe, CTO Greg Fiedler, and CISO Andy Ritter, under Secretary Neil Weaver.
